fault_hypothesis: The overlap detection on line 67 uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`) which incorrectly allows intervals that touch at a boundary to be scheduled. The non-overlap condition should use strict inequalities (`<`, `>`) to correctly identify such cases as conflicts.

spec.expr: (end <= s or start >= e) == (end < s or start > e)

constraints.intent: The non-overlap condition should use strict inequalities for touching intervals.
If no conflict is detected for the current item, the new interval must be strictly before or after it.

spec.expr: all(interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If the schedule is updated, the new interval must not have a boundary conflict with any existing interval.

spec.expr: not __cbfl_result[0] or all(interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If an interval is successfully scheduled, it must not touch any existing intervals.
